note: SNG-089 (Erik-directed 2026-07-12): 'some traditions simply aren't going to resonate with human civilization as well — is the chaos tradition seated in the fantasy realm area? Is concrete near machine and mostly dwarves and precursor stuff going about strange worldscale operations?' **He was reading the ring correctly. The clusters fall straight out of its geometry.**
principle: KIND is not a species menu. It is what a disposition BECOMES when a people lives inside it for a thousand years. The ring predicts kind; kind does not decorate the ring.

clusters.name: The Deep Works
peoples: mason [concrete] — DWARVEN. Stone, deep halls, the Weighing Yard. They mine INTO the Precursor substrate and have for longer than anyone has been counting.
enginewright [mechanical] — PART-MACHINE. Not cyborgs; something slower. They tend the Great Engine and the Engine has been tending them back.
horizon [space] — the only people who cross between. Nomads, and the Deep Works' only reliable messengers.
clusters.why: All three sit on PRECURSOR BONES. The service-ways run under everything — under the Valley, under the Gearlands, under places the Wrights have not admitted to.
clusters.theOperation: ⛔ They are conducting WORLDSCALE OPERATIONS that nobody — including them — entirely understands. Something down there has been running its original errand for four hundred years and has recently begun running FASTER. The Masons weigh; the Enginewrights maintain; the Horizon-Walkers carry. **None of them will say what the work is for, and the honest answer is that they do not know, and they are afraid to stop.**

clusters.name: The Wild Half
peoples: churnfolk [chaos] — FAE. Nothing twice, nothing where you left it. Joyous, generous, and lethally unreliable.
abyssal [demonic] — HORNED, and honest about appetite in a way the pious cannot manage. They bargain fairly. That is the horror.
unmaker [destruction] — who clear what the wild has finished with. The Wild Half's undertakers, and its conscience.
clusters.why: Demons, fae, and ruin, standing in a row on the ring. **That is not a coincidence — it is the ring telling you what those dispositions BECOME when they are lived.**
clusters.theOperation: The Wild Half does not conspire. It ACCRETES. The Churn Edge creeps; the Maw deepens; the Scouring works outward. **Nobody is driving. That is precisely what frightens the Lattice.**
clusters.substrate: THE TANGLED CURRENT — wild nanites AND living current together, kept by no one. Where the Continuous keep the fabricated deep in disciplined form (SNG-131) and the Rootkin tend the living current, the Wild Half channels BOTH, undisciplined. This is why it accretes rather than obeys: the two substrates tangled and unkept do not take orders, they improvise. Potent and 'lethally unreliable' — nothing twice, nothing where you left it. The third keeper-kind: not a pole opposite the others, a different RELATIONSHIP to the deep (tangled/wild vs kept).

clusters.name: The Continuous
peoples: seraphic [angelic] — ⛔ THEY NEVER ABANDONED THEIR POWER AT THE TRANSITION. They are the only people CONTINUOUS with the world that was.
clusters.why: Every other people is post-Transition — reconstructed, reinvented, or grown from the wreckage. **The Seraphic Orders simply kept going.**
clusters.theOperation: They are OLD, they REMEMBER, and their certainty is not arrogance — **it is memory.** When a Seraph tells you what is right, they are not reasoning from principles. They are recalling. **And they are the only ones left who can tell you what the world was like before, which means they are the only ones who can lie about it.**

clusters.name: The Dissolving
peoples: numinous [spiritual] — no longer entirely present. Their deepest are not people anymore in any sense a Mason would accept.
clusters.why: They dissolved most of their material life into practice, and the practice worked.
clusters.theOperation: **They built the waygates.** Nobody else could have. And they will not say why, and they do not use them.

humanMajority: The Valley, the Marches, the Stillhold, the Quickwood, the Palelands, the Reasoned Hold, the Feeling Coast, the Lattice-Cities, the Veiled Reach, the Stark Reach, the Pattern Reach, the Kept Reach, the Making, and the two light/dark peoples are HUMAN — differently shaped by disposition, but human. **Kind is the exception; disposition is the rule.**
